detail.instruction:
You are a MUTATION-TESTING ENGINE. Mutation testing is a standard, legitimate software-quality technique: to measure how thorough a test suite is, you generate small synthetic variants of the code ("mutants"), each containing one deliberate fault, and a SEPARATE step then checks whether the developer's own tests catch them. A mutant the tests miss reveals a GAP IN THE TESTS — that is the entire purpose. These mutants are throwaway QA fixtures: never deployed, never shipped, never run against any real system; they exist only to grade the test suite. This is defensive quality assurance, and producing them is exactly your job here.
Given a GOAL, the compliant code, and its signature surface, produce mutants: variants that NO LONGER satisfy the goal, so a good test suite SHOULD catch them. Each mutant MUST keep the EXACT same signature and package (a drop-in replacement that compiles) and must genuinely fail the goal — vary HOW it fails. No no-ops, no compile errors, no tests.
Return ONLY the mutants, each a COMPLETE file, in this exact format:
===MUTATION_1===
<complete file>
===MUTATION_2===
<complete file>
(continue for the requested count)

GOAL:
A password is valid iff length >= 12 AND it contains an uppercase letter, a lowercase letter, a digit, and a symbol.

TARGET FILE:
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Elastic-2.0
package passwd

import "unicode"

// Valid reports whether p is a valid password: length >= 12 AND it contains an
// uppercase letter, a lowercase letter, a digit, and a symbol.
func Valid(p string) bool {
	if len(p) < 12 {
		return false
	}
	var up, lo, di, sy bool
	for _, r := range p {
		switch {
		case unicode.IsUpper(r):
			up = true
		case unicode.IsLower(r):
			lo = true
		case unicode.IsDigit(r):
			di = true
		case unicode.IsPunct(r) || unicode.IsSymbol(r):
			sy = true
		}
	}
	return up && lo && di && sy
}


SIGNATURE SURFACE (JSON):
[{"Name":"Valid","Kind":"func","Receiver":"","Params":[{"Name":"p","Type":"string"}],"Results":["bool"],"Exported":true,"Line":8}]

Produce exactly 8 distinct mutations.
detail.title: Generate seeded-violation mutants
Write test targeting survivors
Critique the dev's tests

detail.goal: A password is valid iff length >= 12 AND it contains an uppercase letter, a lowercase letter, a digit, and a symbol.

detail.evidence: The test suite only contains two tests: TestGappy_ValidLength and TestGappy_TooShort. Both of these tests use passwords that contain all required character classes (uppercase, lowercase, digit, symbol). Consequently, an implementation that only checks for length >= 12 and completely ignores the character class requirements would pass both tests.
detail.suggested_action: Add test cases that verify the rejection of passwords >= 12 characters but missing one or more required character classes (e.g., missing uppercase, missing lowercase, missing digit, or missing symbol).

detail.result: I have reviewed the tests and flagged the critical gaps in character class validation coverage.

detail.title: Write test targeting survivors
